description: Creates an interactive pan button that pans the 2D graph viewport to center on a coordinate, point, or geometric object. Requires at() as the first argument for button placement.
utility: Create an interactive button that pans the graph viewport to a target point or object

arguments.name: position
arguments.description: Logical position for the pan button using at(row, col)

arguments.name: target
arguments.description: Target to center on: x,y coordinates, a point variable, or an object with a center (circle, polygon)

alternates.description: Button that pans to center on the given x, y coordinates
Button that pans to center on a previously defined point
Button that pans to center on a circle, polygon, or other object

examples.expression:
G = g2d(at(2,2), 20, 20, range(-5, 5), range(-5, 5), type(plotting))
pan(at(25,2), G, 5, 5)

examples.scenario: Pan button to a point variable
examples.expression:
G = g2d(at(2,2), 20, type(geometry))
P = point(G, 7, 3)
pan(at(25,2), G, P)

examples.scenario: Pan button to center on a circle
examples.expression:
C = circle(G, 3, point(G, 5, 5))
pan(at(25,2), G, C)
